reviews.review_msg: She is one of the worst. Very Sarcastic and try not to take class from her
She is very sarcastic and doesn't care about her students. Do not take this course with her

Straight shooter. Very clear what she wants. You get what you put in. However, the assignments can be very tedious and time-consuming even though they are not worth much. Exams are exactly like practice questions, and her lectures are full of many hints.
The course was pretty easy.  Very little homework and the exams were straight from the course notes.  I skipped several lectures but did just fine.

Good prof! The course material isn't particularly exciting, but Lorene makes it meaningful and practical. She's passionate about the subject and is approachable for help. The midterm was a challenge but it's still fair; you'll have to work for the grade, and make sure you attend lectures.

I like her as a prof because she's very clear on what she wants and you literally just need to study the notes she gives. ALL her questions are based off her notes so make sure you attend class. First half of the class is more difficult so pay more attention. Second half is easy because she gives you one specific way to do each type of question.

One of the tougher courses I have done. Not many assignments and a low work load, gives lots of practice questions which are essential to doing well. Have to put a lot more work into this course than others. Assignments are worth little compared to the work put into them. Tests were challenging, need to study lots. I enjoyed the content though.

I loved Lorene's class. Her notes were fill in the blank with so many types of examples. She gave hints to what questions were on exams and as the term went along I enjoyed the material. She is very straight up so I understand how some people can be intimidated. Speak with her in person for feedback. I left this course having learned a lot.
She is condescending and a poor lecturer. Textbook is practically useless. The final is tough! Make sure to practice lecture problems and weekly problem sets to prepare for final.
great professor like her lectureno need text book. morepractice higher grade you will get
You know Andrew should be grateful that when I took this course I was an adult because had I taken it freshmen year it would be considered CHILD ABUSE!
not too bad.

Worst prof I've ever had. I am willing to overlook the accent, but regardless of the accent he is a terrible teacher.  Fortunately the course is easy, did not attend a whole class after the first midterm and still got an A-.  He did make the final ridiculously tricky.  Super rigid about marks,way too worried about cheating. Truly terrible professor

Despite his problems in English, if you ask him for help he will take the time to reinterpret the material! He's so enthusiastic which will honestly make you love  the class! His tests are easy -no surprises, just do the homework and read his notes. Awesome teacher!
HONESTLY, GET ANOTHER PROF..

Unclear, he sounds like Kermit the frog with an Indian accent, couldn't understand even when sitting at the front. Also rambles way too much about cheating, used half a lecture and got pissed off when students asked questions about it. His notes were based on an older version of the text so you'd have to find out on your on when to stop reading...

Took 3 other classes with him. He goes on with his life stories a lot, but a lot of times, they connect the course material to real life. You will definitely have to do readings and learning by yourself to succeed in the class. Bernie is open for help  if you go for it.
Great prof.  Lots of entertainment value in the lectures.
Great prof, really cares about his students. Willing to go way out of his way to help students and has probably the fairest tests out of any class I've taken.

He flies through lectures so you must preread. However, he keeps the mood of the class light. He is extremely nice and goes out of his way to help you if you need it. Best professor i've had. His exams test based off the learning curve so do lots of practice. I'd love to take another class with him.
best prof ever, super sarcastic, but very helpful, very fair, tries his best to avoid cheating in his pop quizzes, will do attendance so better show up for class

Tends to fly through lecture slides very quickly, so do pre-readings! Goes over sample problems thoroughly. Will spend time doing lecture-irrelevant things, such as stories and things that should be done during office hours. Holds many review sessions. Exams must be done at a relatively fast pace. You'll have a love-hate relationship with Bernie.
Nice guy, terrible prof. Forced you to attend his useless lecture because of his quizzes. Avoid at all costs.

Not a very good teacher. He tends to move through the lecture notes very quickly, but forces you to attend because he has random quizzes. Despite his lack of teaching abilities he's definitely a nice, very intelligent guy; he's just not cut out for teaching, wouldn't recommend.
Best prof ever.
Terrible lecturer, he's a cool dude but he can't teach. Flies through the slides and expects that you learn from the book. Do not recommend!!
So far, one of the best professor I have ever had at sfu. He is a Funny guy, and very helpful. attend each class and study for quizes for A+.I didnt go to class but studied and ended up with a B.

Holds review sessions before exams and genuinely cares for his students. Keeps a good balance between learning and fun. As much as smaller class sizes would be helpful I found he did a wonderful job teaching the material by doing actual problems. Assignments, quizzes, and exams are fair but still tough so keep up. Definitely recommended.

By far the most interesting Prof i have ever had, he is very funny. Distributes candies and others things in class. He is very helpful too, a but busy guy because he teaches 4-5 classes. Take pop quizes so attendance is mandatory. If u are a very student he is a very guy to take course with, he helps a lot.

Spends most of the two hour class telling stories about his glory days - however has surprise pop quizzes so attendance is mandatory. Has a bit of an attitude and gets overwhelmed by his workload and responds by refusing to answer emails, etc. Nice guy, not a great prof for this course, probably smaller classes.

By far the best professor I have ever had. Very helpful. The class is hard, but it is stats after all. He makes it as simple as possible. You still have to work for your grade, but if you do, it shows. An upstanding guy and an incredible asset to have at SFU. If you can take any class with him, do it!
Stand up guy, who can appreciate the struggles of a student.  Class act.
Great guy, hes awesome and best prof ever. Take this course with him. You will not be disappointed. - Vincent
Very easy class if you make the effort to study. Online assignments are super easy, overall great prof! Also very funny!

Carlie is the best among the other buec 232 profs. She's a bit of a ditz sometimes LOOL but still teaches the concepts well. For a 4-credit course, 232 had very little workload (2 assignments, midterm, and final). Midterm average is high so study hard.

She is a good prof to take BUEC 232 with. She tells what concepts are useful and what are not in real life. She gives a clear idea of the concepts she will test in exams. Focuses on the understanding of the concepts and tests the same in the exams. Missed few classes and did majority of the studying last week still managed to do fairly good.

This is her first time teaching in SFU Spring 2019. She is kind and caring about her students. She tries her best to help students with questions about materials. Its 4 credits so just lots of stuff to learn if you havent taken any stat course. Honestly, recommend taking class with her cz she makes things easy for you to learn

Her lecture notes are confusing compared to another stat course I took in the past. I hope she can go through examples by hand rather than just presenting them on the slides. She does not provide practice questions, instead, you have to resort to Wileyplus (which sucks!!!) Questions from this site are confusing in terms of wording and length.

She is prof who gives you real-life advice and which is helpful. This is the first time she is taking a course at SFU Spring 2019, but she is fantastic. She tries hard to make it easy for the student. BUEC 232 is a four credit course. The course load is usually substantial. It is highly recommended to take a class with her.
Chris is smart and his lecture is clear and easy to understand.
good and nice
Explains everything crystal clear with a good sense of humour. Loved the early dismissals. Exams are easy as long as you study.
Chris really cares about the success of his students. He rarely wasted time on meaningless definitions and focused on the examinable material. I would highly suggest taking this course with him.

I never thought I'd enjoy stats, but if you take it with this guy, you will! He is very helpful, and his lectures are to the point and very clear. He doesn't waste your time either, usually lets class out early. I would highly recommend him as a prof, throughly enjoyed his class and teaching!

This guy is hilarious. Early dismissal of almost 30 mins were awesome. The course itself is bunk. I don't know why they even bother having a "business economics" subject when this was practically stats. Might as well just put it name it a BUS column seeing as how it just looks like they want to charge more money.
I like chris, he is a good prof. I for sure recommend him. Also he is really funny and he makes his class interesting. Don't buy text book. I never used it. Overall quality is A

Definitely one of the best profs I've ever had. Keeps you engaged during lecture, very good at explaining concepts, and usually lets you out half hour early. Highly recommended.  Fair exams. He is definitely "irreplaceable".

This prof is AMAZING. Definitely the best I've had thus far at SFU. I never really used the textbook as his notes were really good and he's just an overall funny and fair grader. Finally a prof who actually seems to care how his students do in his class. Go figure.
best teacher i've ever had, hands down. highly, highly, highly recommend. If i could have him as my prof for every class i would be willing to pay double tuition
Very nice!
Great teacher. Easy to understand, good notes. Textbook isn't used too much... he supplies his own questions and gives you time to work on them in class. Very open to questions.
